article_title: 'Cool in 90 seconds' - the fake portable air conditioners sweeping the internet

tasks.prompt: What problem does the article investigate?
options: Fake online adverts for small portable air conditioners
A shortage of winter heaters in supermarkets
A new law banning all fans in the UK
NASA selling official home air conditioners
tasks.correct_answer: Fake online adverts for small portable air conditioners
tasks.explanation_hebrew: הכתבה בודקת פרסומות מטעות למכשירים קטנים שמוצגים כמזגנים ניידים.

tasks.prompt: What cooling promise appears in the headline and adverts?
options: Cut humidity to zero in 90 hours
tasks.explanation_hebrew: הטענה הבולטת היא קירור מהיר מאוד תוך 90 שניות.

tasks.prompt: Which platforms are mentioned as places where these adverts spread?
tasks.explanation_hebrew: הפרסומות הופצו ברשתות ופלטפורמות כמו Facebook ו-YouTube.

tasks.prompt: What price range did the BBC report for many of these devices?
tasks.explanation_hebrew: הכתבה מציינת שמכשירים כאלה נמכרו לעיתים במחיר של כ-70 עד 120 ליש״ט.

tasks.prompt: Why are consumers more likely to notice or buy these products during heatwaves? Answer in 2–4 sentences.
tasks.rubric_hebrew: התשובה צריכה להתבסס על פרטים אמיתיים מן הכתבה, להיות ברורה, ולא להעתיק משפטים שלמים.
expected_points: High temperatures increase demand for cooling products
Trusted fans or air conditioners can sell out
Urgency makes exaggerated adverts more tempting
Consumers may not check claims carefully when uncomfortable

tasks.prompt: Explain what “misleading adverts” means in this article, using two details from the report. Answer in 2–4 sentences.
tasks.rubric_hebrew: התשובה צריכה להתבסס על פרטים אמיתיים מן הכתבה, להיות ברורה, ולא להעתיק משפטים שלמים.
expected_points: The adverts create a false impression about cooling power
Examples include dramatic 90-second claims or NASA-style backstories
Fake reviews/testimonials or inconsistent branding can mislead buyers

tasks.prompt: Why does Stuart Matthews taking a product apart matter to the story?
options: It shows the inside was too simple to match the advert’s claims
It proves all expensive air conditioners are fake
It shows cardboard fins are standard in real air conditioners
It confirms NASA built the device for homes
tasks.correct_answer: It shows the inside was too simple to match the advert’s claims
tasks.explanation_hebrew: פירוק המכשיר מדגים פער בין ההבטחות לבין הרכיבים הפשוטים בפועל.

tasks.prompt: Write an 80–120 word summary of the article without copying sentences.
tasks.rubric_hebrew: התשובה צריכה להתבסס על פרטים אמיתיים מן הכתבה, להיות ברורה, ולא להעתיק משפטים שלמים.
expected_points: Fake portable air-conditioner adverts spread online
Claims include very rapid cooling and impressive backstories
Examples include weak components, water tanks, fake reviews, or exaggerated testimonials
The ASA warns consumers to be sceptical and report suspicious adverts

tasks.prompt: What red flags should a reader look for before buying a “portable air conditioner” from an online advert? Answer in 2–4 sentences.
tasks.rubric_hebrew: התשובה צריכה להתבסס על פרטים אמיתיים מן הכתבה, להיות ברורה, ולא להעתיק משפטים שלמים.
expected_points: Grand backstories such as former NASA engineers
Inconsistent branding, AI-style errors, or unclear seller details

tasks.prompt: Write a short consumer warning based on the article. Include one specific claim and one practical action.
tasks.rubric_hebrew: התשובה צריכה להתבסס על פרטים אמיתיים מן הכתבה, להיות ברורה, ולא להעתיק משפטים שלמים.
expected_points: Mentions a specific claim such as cooling in 90 seconds
Advises checking trusted sellers, reviews, specifications, or reporting to the ASA
